title: AMD unveils Helios rack and MI455X accelerator in bid to close the gap with Nvidia
excerpt: AMD unveiled a suite of new data centre products at its Advancing AI event in San Francisco on Thursday, claiming they will outperform Nvidia’s competing hardware across AI training and inference. The announcements included the MI455X AI accelerator, the Helios server rack that packs 72 of those chips into a single system, and the Venice […]
content_html:
TL;DR

AMD launched MI455X chips, Helios racks, and Venice Epyc CPUs at Advancing AI, with OpenAI and Cerebras pledging to deploy the hardware at scale

AMD unveiled a suite of new data centre products at its Advancing AI event in San Francisco on Thursday, claiming they will outperform Nvidia’s competing hardware across AI training and inference. The announcements included the MI455X AI accelerator, the Helios server rack that packs 72 of those chips into a single system, and the Venice generation of Epyc server processors built on TSMC’s two-nanometre process. AMD predicted the total market it is pursuing will reach $2 trillion by 2030, with AI accelerators alone accounting for more than a trillion dollars of that figure.

Investors were unimpressed, sending AMD shares down about four percent during the presentation despite the stock having more than doubled in value this year. The scepticism reflects the gap between AMD’s ambitions and Nvidia’s dominance: Nvidia’s Vera Rubin platform is already in full production and shipping to customers including OpenAI, CoreWeave, and Microsoft. AMD CEO Lisa Su has delivered a remarkable growth run, but Wall Street’s expectations were already baked into a stock that had risen roughly 145 percent year to date heading into the event.

Su brought executives from major AI companies on stage to endorse AMD’s hardware. OpenAI’s Sachin Katti said his company expects to deploy Helios “at massive scale” as it scrambles to add infrastructure capacity. The event came a day after AMD announced it would invest up to $5 billion in Anthropic and deploy two gigawatts of MI450 series GPUs in Helios racks to run Claude, with the first gigawatt shipping in the first half of 2027.

AMD also announced a partnership with Cerebras, whose wafer-scale chips specialise in ultra-fast AI inference, to combine Helios racks with Cerebras servers for customers who need the lowest possible latency. AMD hardware will handle the work of deciphering queries while Cerebras chips provide rapid answers. Cerebras CEO Andrew Feldman said the combined product will ship from Cerebras-owned data centres starting in the fourth quarter and will beat a similar offering Nvidia is assembling from its recent Groq acquisition.

The Venice Epyc processor, meanwhile, is AMD’s answer to Nvidia’s push into server CPUs with its Vera chip, which Nvidia has claimed gives it a performance edge. AMD said Venice will keep it well ahead of Vera, setting up a direct contest that independent benchmarks have yet to settle. The rivalry now spans accelerators, server processors, and complete rack-scale systems, with both companies pitching end-to-end data centre solutions rather than individual components.

content_html_zh:
TL;DR

AMD 在 Advancing AI 大会上推出了 MI455X 芯片、Helios 机架和 Venice Epyc CPU，OpenAI 和 Cerebras 承诺大规模部署硬件

AMD 周四在旧金山举行的 Advancing AI 活动上推出了一套新的数据中心产品，声称它们在 AI 训练和推理方面将超越 Nvidia 的竞争硬件。 这些公告包括 MI455X AI 加速器、将 72 个芯片封装到单个系统中的 Helios 服务器机架，以及基于台积电两纳米工艺构建的 Venice 一代 Epyc 服务器处理器。 AMD 预测，到 2030 年，其所追求的市场总额将达到 2 万亿美元，其中仅人工智能加速器就占到了超过 1 万亿美元。

投资者并不为所动，尽管 AMD 的股价今年已经上涨了一倍多，但在演示期间，AMD 的股价还是下跌了约 4%。 这种怀疑反映了 AMD 的雄心与 Nvidia 的主导地位之间的差距：Nvidia 的 Vera Rubin 平台已经全面投入生产，并向 OpenAI、CoreWeave 和微软等客户发货。 AMD 首席执行官苏姿丰 (Lisa Su) 实现了显着的增长，但华尔街的预期已经融入了该股，该股今年迄今已上涨约 145%。

苏召集了主要人工智能公司的高管上台为 AMD 的硬件背书。 OpenAI 的 Sachin Katti 表示，他的公司希望在努力增加基础设施容量的同时“大规模”部署 Helios。 就在该事件发生的前一天，AMD 宣布将向 Anthropic 投资高达 50 亿美元，并在 Helios 机架中部署 2 千兆瓦的 MI450 系列 GPU 来运行 Claude，第一千兆瓦的 GPU 将于 2027 年上半年发货。

AMD 还宣布与 Cerebras 合作，将 Helios 机架与 Cerebras 服务器结合起来，为需要尽可能低延迟的客户提供服务。 Cerebras 的晶圆级芯片专注于超快速人工智能推理。 AMD 硬件将处理破译查询的工作，而 Cerebras 芯片则提供快速答案。 Cerebras 首席执行官 Andrew Feldman 表示，合并后的产品将从第四季度开始从 Cerebras 拥有的数据中心发货，并将击败 Nvidia 从最近收购的 Groq 中组装的类似产品。

与此同时，Venice Epyc 处理器是 AMD 针对 Nvidia 凭借 Vera 芯片进军服务器 CPU 领域的回应，Nvidia 声称该芯片为其带来了性能优势。 AMD 表示，Venice 将远远领先于 Vera，从而展开一场直接竞争，但独立基准测试尚未解决。 现在的竞争涵盖加速器、服务器处理器和完整的机架规模系统，两家公司都推出端到端数据中心解决方案，而不是单个组件。
